title: otanthus maritimus
intro: Otanthus maritimus, also known as Sea Whitlow-grass, is a low-growing, perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and minor cuts. The health benefits of Otanthus maritimus include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for topical application or internal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
